title: Optimistic loading across app via React Query cache-first mutations with rollback

requirements.summary: Move optimistic UI behavior into React Query mutation hooks (cache-first updates + rollback) with targeted invalidation only when required
acceptanceCriteria: For each of these actions—post like/unlike, comment add, comment like/unlike, follow/unfollow, post create/delete, profile save—the UI updates immediately without waiting for the backend response.
If a mutation fails, the UI state is reverted to the previous value (no permanent ‘stuck’ optimistic state).
Mutations do not broadly invalidate unrelated queries; invalidation is targeted (e.g., only the affected query keys) and only used when an optimistic cache update is insufficient.
The app still reaches eventual consistency with backend data after mutations complete.

file_operations.description: Refactor key mutations to implement consistent optimistic updates using React Query mutation lifecycle: add onMutate to synchronously update relevant cached query data (e.g., ['feed'], ['userPosts', principal], ['comments', postId], ['currentUserProfile'], ['userProfile', principalId], and optionally ['post', postId]); capture and return snapshot context; add onError rollback restoring snapshot; add onSettled/onSuccess to perform minimal targeted invalidation/refetch only for eventual consistency where cache updates cannot be made fully authoritative (e.g., inbox/aggregate counts).

Standardize mutation inputs so hooks have enough information to optimistically update cache (e.g., include current user principal for like/unlike/follow state, include postId for comment ops, include author principal and optimistic temp IDs for post create). Ensure optimistic cache updates are applied consistently across all entry points.

requirements.summary: Update UI components to rely on unified optimistic React Query cache state instead of per-component local optimistic state across all entry points
acceptanceCriteria: Liking a post from `PostCard` and from `PostDetailModal` shows the same immediate like/like-count changes and the same rollback behavior on error.
Comment like/unlike shows immediate count/icon updates in both `CommentsModal` and `PostDetailModal` comment lists, with rollback on error.
Adding a comment shows the new comment immediately in the list (optimistically) and removes it or marks it failed if the backend request fails.
Follow/unfollow updates follower/following state immediately on profile pages and reverts on error.

file_operations.description: Remove per-component optimistic like state (local optimisticLiked/optimisticLikeCount) and rely exclusively on React Query cached post state for liked/like-count rendering. Wire like/unlike interactions to the unified optimistic mutation hooks so the feed card reflects immediate cache updates and rollback automatically.

Refactor modal to render from React Query cached post state (rather than a static Post prop) so optimistic updates from anywhere (feed card, profile grid, inbox) are reflected immediately. Update like/unlike, add comment, comment like/unlike, and delete flows to rely on unified optimistic mutation hooks and cached state for immediate UI + rollback behavior.

Ensure comments list rendering and like counts rely on the React Query ['comments', postId] cache state; remove/avoid any per-item local optimistic state. Use unified optimistic add-comment and like/unlike-comment mutation hooks so both CommentsModal and PostDetailModal show identical immediate updates and rollback behavior.

Update post detail opening logic to pass a post identifier (or otherwise ensure PostDetailModal renders from cached query state) so optimistic like/comment/delete changes are consistent and visible when opening from the profile posts grid.

Ensure follow/unfollow button and counts rely on the optimistic cache-updated profiles (['currentUserProfile'] and ['userProfile', principalId]) and that PostDetailModal usage renders from cached post state for consistent optimistic behavior when opened from the creator profile grid.

Update PostDetailModal usage (if passing a Post object today) to pass a post identifier / render from cached query state so optimistic likes/comments/deletes behave identically when opening the same post from inbox activity.

requirements.summary: Make post create/delete feel instant by optimistically updating feed and user posts caches (['feed'] and ['userPosts', principal]) with rollback
acceptanceCriteria: After creating a post, the new post appears immediately in the feed and in the author’s profile posts list (optimistically).
After deleting a post, the post disappears immediately from the feed and from the author’s profile posts list (optimistically).
If create/delete fails, the feed/profile lists revert to the prior state.
No full-page loading spinners are required to see the updated feed/profile after these actions; the perceived latency is minimized.

file_operations.description: Implement optimistic create/delete post behavior: onMutate for create inserts an optimistic post into ['feed'] and ['userPosts', authorPrincipal] (using a temporary id and the provided image/caption/author/timestamp), and onSuccess reconciles the temporary id with the returned real id; onError rolls back prior cached lists. For delete, onMutate removes the post from ['feed'], ['userPosts', authorPrincipal], and any cached ['post', postId]; onError restores snapshots. Keep invalidation narrowly scoped (e.g., only ['inbox'] or aggregates like ['totalLikes', principal] if needed for eventual consistency).

Pass the current authenticated principal into the create-post mutation variables (so the hook can optimistically update ['userPosts', principal] as well as ['feed']). Keep the perceived latency minimized by relying on the optimistic cache insertion rather than waiting for feed refetch to reflect the new post after navigation.

Ensure profile posts grid reflects optimistic create/delete changes from the ['userPosts', principal] cache immediately (no manual full refetch logic), and that the selected post detail rendering stays in sync with cache after optimistic create/delete completes or rolls back.
